title: The search box became a writer
summary: Google rebuilt its search box on Gemini 3.5 Flash, generating answer pages instead of link lists; Anthropic's interpretability team found an emergent 'global workspace' inside Claude that can be read and even edited; and a cheap pruning step trims a third off RAG serving costs. Plus a sweep of AI-infrastructure, open-weight, and security news.

topics: Google Search rebuilt on Gemini 3.5 Flash
RAG context pruning with a cheap grader model

repeat_coverage.new_development: New model codenames Sol/Terra/Luna revealed and initial access limited to users the U.S. government selects.

dives.story: Google Search rebuilt on Gemini 3.5 Flash
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone who uses the web is affected: the dominant search product now answers with a generated page instead of links, changing both how people get information and the click economy that funds the open web.

Interpretability researchers and safety teams get a causal, editable readout of a model's internal reasoning before it reaches output — a concrete handhold for detecting deception, not just watching it.
Anyone running retrieval in production can cut ~34% off per-query cost by inserting a cheap grader that reads the whole retrieved set and drops redundant chunks — a practical, replicable win.

concepts_taught: reading a model's internal 'global workspace' (the Jacobian-lens interpretability method)
pruning RAG retrieved context with a cheap grader that scores the whole set at once
